title: marcgraviastrum gigantophyllum
intro: Marcgraviastrum gigantophyllum, commonly known as the giant Marcgravia, is a plant species in the family Marcgraviaceae. It is a woody climber that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, known for its unique, umbrella-shaped leaves. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, attributed to its therapeutic actions such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Marcgraviastrum gigantophyllum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
